input.background: Refer to Example 5.4 to calculate the standard deviations of the sampling distributions of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ and the sample median $m$. Additionally, determine which statistic appears to be a better estimator of the population mean $\mu$.
input.question: Which statistic appears to be the better estimator for the population mean $\mu$, the sample mean $\bar{x}$ or the sample median $m$?

output.answer: The sampling distribution of the sample median $m$ is biased (i.e., it is located to the left of the mean $\mu$), and its standard deviation $\sigma_m=1.95$ is larger than the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}, \sigma_{\bar{x}}=1.64$. Consequently, for the population in question, the sample mean $\bar{x}$ would be a better estimator of the population mean $\mu$ than the sample median $m$ would be.

meta_info.caption: Variance of Estimators -Craps Application
meta_info.section: 5.2 Properties of Sampling Distributions: Unbiasedness and Minimum Variance
